text: Planning my trip to Japan took months of research, from booking flights to choosing hostels in Tokyo and Kyoto. I was most excited about experiencing a new culture—its temples, train etiquette, and quiet streets felt completely different from home. Food was an adventure too: I tried ramen from tiny stalls, fresh sushi at markets, and sweet mochi I still crave. Language barriers were tricky at times. I once ended up at the wrong restaurant by misreading a menu, but the staff were patient, helping me order with hand gestures. Despite the confusion, those unexpected moments became my favorite memories. Travel taught me that even mistakes lead to stories worth sharing.
